component.name: SideNavigation
component.description: The global app shell rail down the left edge of the layout: a Huffpost logo header with a collapse toggle, a scrollable list of collapsible sections of leaf nav items, and a pinned user footer. Driven declaratively by a `sections: NavSection[]` array; controlled via `activeItemId` + `onNavigate`. Exactly one per app layout.
component.note: White rail (neutral.0), 1px neutral.200 right border, sharp corners (radius.none). 280px expanded vs 56px (w-14) icon-only collapsed, with a width transition. Three regions: header (logo + appTitle + collapse toggle), scrollable sections, pinned user footer. Only leaf items carry the active state — forest.50 fill, forest.700 text, bold, 2px forest.700 right border + aria-current='page'; section headings are never active but inherit the active tint on their icon in collapsed mode when they contain the active leaf. Composes the Icon component for chevrons, section/leaf glyphs, add, and more-horizontal.

rail.width: 280px expanded / 56px (w-14) collapsed, with a width transition 0.2s ease-in-out
rail.layout: flex column, full height; header and footer flex-shrink 0, sections flex-1 overflow-y auto

header.layout: flex row, align-items center; gap spacing.base (8px) + px spacing.sm (12px) expanded / centered, no padding collapsed
header.title: Inter bold 14px / 20px, color.neutral.800; truncates; hidden when collapsed
header.toggle: 24px icon button (chevron-left expanded / chevron-right collapsed), color.neutral.500 → color.neutral.800 on hover, hover bg color.neutral.50

section.heading: full-width button, Inter semibold 14px / 20px, min-height 32px; gap spacing.base (8px), px spacing.md (16px), py spacing.base (8px) expanded / centered collapsed
section.text: color.neutral.800 at rest; hover bg color.neutral.50
section.icon: 20px Icon, color.neutral.500 at rest; color.forest.700 when the section is active-tinted in collapsed mode
section.add: 16px 'add' glyph, color.neutral.500 → color.neutral.800 on hover, hover bg color.neutral.100 (expanded only, when onAdd is set)
section.collapsedActiveTint: color.forest.50 fill, color.forest.700 text/icon, bold, 2px color.forest.700 right border — only when the collapsed section contains the active leaf

leaf.layout: full-width button in a role='list' <ul>, gap spacing.base (8px), pl spacing.7xl (48px) / pr spacing.md (16px), py spacing.base (8px), min-height 32px
leaf.text: Inter 14px / 20px, color.neutral.800 semibold at rest; hover bg color.neutral.50
leaf.icon: 20px Icon, color.neutral.500 at rest; color.forest.700 when active
leaf.active: color.forest.50 fill, color.forest.700 text + icon, bold, 2px color.forest.700 right border, aria-current='page'

footer.layout: flex row, align-items center; gap spacing.base (8px), px spacing.md (16px), py spacing.sm (12px) expanded / centered collapsed
footer.avatar: 20px forest.500 circle (radius.pill), white initials 10px semibold (auto-derived from name when initials omitted)
footer.name: Inter semibold 14px / 20px, color.neutral.800; truncates
footer.overflow: 24px 'more-horizontal' icon button, color.neutral.500 → color.neutral.800 on hover (expanded only, when onUserMenuOpen is set)

tokens.transition: width 0.2s ease-in-out (rail); background-color & color 0.12s (rows, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion)
tokens.focusRing: 2px color.forest.500 outline, inset (offset -2px so the ring stays within the row)

useCases: The global left rail of an authenticated app layout — the navigation shell paired with the main content area
Switch between top-level destinations (Dashboard, Articles, Settings) via leaf items, with the current page tinted
Group destinations under collapsible sections so a large nav stays scannable
Collapse to a 56px icon-only rail to reclaim horizontal space on a content-heavy view
Expose a per-section add (+) affordance for 'new item in this group' (e.g. add a collection)
Show the signed-in user in a pinned footer with an overflow menu for account actions

commonPatterns.name: App-shell rail with an active page
commonPatterns.description: The standard shell: two or three sections of leaf items, the current leaf marked via activeItemId, navigation handled by onNavigate.
commonPatterns.jsx: <SideNavigation activeItemId={page} onNavigate={setPage} sections={[{ id: 'main', label: 'Workspace', icon: 'home', items: [{ id: 'dashboard', label: 'Dashboard', icon: 'grid' }, { id: 'articles', label: 'Articles', icon: 'file-text' }] }, { id: 'admin', label: 'Admin', icon: 'settings', items: [{ id: 'members', label: 'Members' }, { id: 'billing', label: 'Billing' }] }]} />

commonPatterns.name: Controlled collapse / expand
commonPatterns.description: Drive the 56px icon-only rail from your own state; the toggle button flips its aria-label between 'Collapse sidebar' and 'Expand sidebar'.
commonPatterns.jsx: <SideNavigation isCollapsed={collapsed} onToggleCollapse={() => setCollapsed((c) => !c)} activeItemId={page} onNavigate={setPage} sections={sections} />

commonPatterns.name: Section with an add (+) affordance
commonPatterns.description: Pass onAdd on a section to render a trailing '+' on its heading row for creating a new item in that group (shown only when expanded).
commonPatterns.jsx: <SideNavigation activeItemId={page} onNavigate={setPage} sections={[{ id: 'collections', label: 'Collections', icon: 'folder', onAdd: createCollection, items: collections.map((c) => ({ id: c.id, label: c.name })) }]} />

commonPatterns.name: User footer with an overflow menu
commonPatterns.description: Pass user to pin a footer block; initials auto-derive from the name, and onUserMenuOpen fires from the '…' button to open an account menu.
commonPatterns.jsx: <SideNavigation activeItemId={page} onNavigate={setPage} user={{ name: 'Ada Lovelace', email: 'ada@huffpost.com' }} onUserMenuOpen={openAccountMenu} sections={sections} />

antiPatterns.scenario: Marking a section heading as the active item
antiPatterns.reason: Section headings only expand/collapse their leaf list; activeItemId resolves to a leaf, and headings never take aria-current.
antiPatterns.alternative: Point activeItemId at a leaf item's id; in collapsed mode the containing section icon inherits the active tint automatically.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using SideNavigation for in-page section switching
antiPatterns.reason: It is a global landmark rail for app destinations, not a control for tabbing between panels within a single page.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Tabs for in-page section switching; reserve SideNavigation for the app shell.

antiPatterns.scenario: Rendering more than one SideNavigation per layout
antiPatterns.reason: It is the single global nav landmark; a second rail produces competing landmarks and a confusing shell.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use one SideNavigation per layout; group additional destinations into more sections.

antiPatterns.scenario: Putting page-level actions (Create, Save, Export) on the rail
antiPatterns.reason: Those are scoped to the current page, not global navigation, and crowd the nav with non-destination controls.
antiPatterns.alternative: Place page-level actions in PageHeader's actions slot; keep the rail for destinations and the per-section onAdd affordance.

slots.header: The Huffpost logo mark + appTitle text + the collapse/expand toggle button; the logo and toggle persist when collapsed, the title hides
slots.sections: The scrollable middle region — a list of collapsible NavSection groups, each a heading button over a role='list' of leaf item buttons
slots.footer: The pinned user block at the bottom — avatar initials + name + email + an overflow '…' button; rendered only when user is passed

commonPartners: PageHeader (the top of the paired content area)
Menu (the surface opened by the footer overflow button)
parentConstraints: Sits as the left rail of the app layout shell, paired with the main content area to its right
Full height of the layout; the content column (typically led by a PageHeader) fills the remaining width

interactions.navigate: Clicking a leaf item fires onNavigate(itemId); the leaf whose id === activeItemId takes the forest active visual and aria-current='page'
interactions.toggleSection: Clicking a section heading expands/collapses its leaf list (aria-expanded + aria-controls); defaultExpanded seeds the initial open state (defaults to open)
interactions.collapse: isCollapsed switches to the 56px icon-only rail with a width transition; leaf lists are hidden and the toggle's aria-label flips to 'Expand sidebar'
interactions.collapsedActive: In collapsed mode a section icon shows the active tint only if it contains the active leaf — the active state bubbles up from leaf to section
interactions.add: A section with onAdd renders a trailing '+' on its heading row (expanded only) that fires onAdd without toggling the section
interactions.userMenu: The footer '…' button fires onUserMenuOpen (typically opening a Menu); avatar initials auto-derive from the user's name when initials is omitted
interactions.hover: Section headings and leaf items at rest tint to neutral.50 background on hover
interactions.focus: 2px forest.500 outline, inset (-2px offset) on every interactive control

behavior.controlled: Navigation is fully controlled: pass activeItemId and update it in onNavigate. Collapse is controlled via isCollapsed + onToggleCollapse. Section open/closed state is held internally (seeded by defaultExpanded); the component owns no navigation or collapse state of its own.

responsive.collapsed: 56px (w-14) icon-only — leaf lists hidden, title and footer text hidden, controls centered
responsive.scroll: the sections region is the only scroll area (flex-1, overflow-y auto); header and footer stay pinned

accessibility.role: navigation — a <nav> landmark named by aria-label (the appTitle); leaf list is a role='list' of buttons
accessibility.ariaCurrent: the active leaf carries aria-current='page'; section headings never do
accessibility.ariaExpanded: section heading buttons expose aria-expanded and aria-controls pointing at their leaf list id (omitted in collapsed mode, where leaf lists are hidden)
accessibility.toggleLabel: the collapse/expand button's aria-label flips between 'Collapse sidebar' (expanded) and 'Expand sidebar' (collapsed); the add affordance is labelled 'Add to {section}' and the footer overflow is labelled 'User menu'
accessibility.focusManagement: natural tab order through header toggle, then each section heading and its visible leaves, then the footer controls; no roving tabindex — every control is tabbable
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Tab / Shift+Tab move through the controls; Enter/Space toggle a section heading or activate a leaf item, toggle collapse, or open the user menu
accessibility.screenReader: Announced as a navigation landmark named by the appTitle; section headings announce their expanded/collapsed state, and the active leaf announces as the current page
accessibility.contrastNote: neutral.800 labels on white, forest.700 active text on the forest.50 tint, and the white avatar initials on forest.500 all meet AA.

athenaConstraints.activeTint: color.forest.50 fill + color.forest.700 text/icon + 2px forest.700 right border (single brand accent)
athenaConstraints.singlePerLayout: exactly one SideNavigation per app layout
athenaConstraints.api: declarative sections array, mirroring the Menu / Tabs array-driven convention

figmaDivergences.implementation: internal useState seeded by defaultExpanded, toggled on heading click
figmaDivergences.reason: Sections must open and close at runtime, so open state is managed in the component rather than fixed per variant.

figmaDivergences.implementation: the toggle button's aria-label flips between 'Collapse sidebar' and 'Expand sidebar'
figmaDivergences.reason: Accessibility addition so the icon-only toggle has a meaningful name in both states.

figmaDivergences.figma: explicit initials in the footer block
figmaDivergences.implementation: initials auto-derive from the user's name when the initials prop is omitted
figmaDivergences.reason: Convenience so callers can pass only name + email and still get an avatar.

aiHints.context: Reach for SideNavigation as the single global left rail of an authenticated app layout, paired with the main content area (whose top is usually a PageHeader). Drive it with a declarative sections array of collapsible groups of leaf items, control the current page with activeItemId + onNavigate, and optionally collapse it to a 56px icon rail via isCollapsed + onToggleCollapse. Only leaf items are active; add a per-section '+' via onAdd and a pinned account block via user + onUserMenuOpen.
neverUseWhen: You need in-page section switching — use Tabs, not the global rail
You want to mark a section heading as the current page — only leaf items take aria-current
A layout already has a SideNavigation — use one per layout, not two
You need page-level actions (Create, Save, Export) — put those in PageHeader, not the nav
